items.title: SQL Injection Remediation Guide
items.summary: Use parameterized query APIs and least-privilege database accounts to prevent SQL injection.
verification_checks: Confirm the rewritten query binds user data through parameters rather than string concatenation.
false_positive_signals: Prepared statements and separate bind variables are already present.
remediation: Replace string-built SQL with prepared statements or parameterized queries.
Validate business constraints separately from SQL escaping.
Use least-privilege DB users and avoid exposing detailed database errors.

items.title: Command Injection Remediation Guide
items.summary: Avoid shell execution for user-controlled data; use safe APIs, argument arrays, allowlists, and sandboxing.
verification_checks: Confirm the fix removes shell=True or untrusted command string construction.
false_positive_signals: The command is already executed as a fixed argument array with shell=False.
remediation: Prefer language/library APIs over external process execution.
Pass arguments as arrays and keep shell=False.
Use strict allowlists for any remaining user-controlled arguments.

items.title: Path Traversal Remediation Guide
items.summary: Canonicalize user-influenced paths and confine them to an allowlisted base directory.
verification_checks: Confirm the fix resolves the path and rejects anything outside the allowlisted base directory.
false_positive_signals: Path is already canonicalized and confined to a safe base directory.
remediation: Resolve to an absolute/real path and verify it is a descendant of an allowlisted base directory.
Map user input to an internal identifier or allowlist instead of using raw filenames.
Reject or strip traversal sequences and null bytes before filesystem access.

items.title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) Remediation Guide
items.summary: Apply context-aware output encoding and keep template auto-escaping enabled for all untrusted data.
verification_checks: Confirm the fix contextually encodes the value or routes it through a vetted sanitizer.
false_positive_signals: Auto-escaping is enabled and the value is not marked safe/raw.
remediation: Use context-aware output encoding (HTML, attribute, JS, URL) at the sink.
Keep framework auto-escaping on and avoid safe/raw/Markup on untrusted data.
For rich HTML, sanitize with a vetted allowlist library (e.g. DOMPurify); add a strict Content-Security-Policy as defense in depth.

items.title: SSRF Remediation Guide
items.summary: Constrain server-side outbound requests with a scheme/host allowlist and block internal and metadata destinations.
verification_checks: Confirm the fix validates the resolved host against an allowlist and blocks internal ranges.
false_positive_signals: Destination is restricted by a strict scheme/host allowlist.
remediation: Allowlist permitted schemes and destination hosts; deny by default.
Resolve the hostname and reject private/link-local/loopback ranges and cloud metadata IPs (169.254.169.254).
Disable unneeded redirects and require egress through a controlled proxy.

items.title: Insecure Deserialization Remediation Guide
items.summary: Avoid deserializing untrusted data with unsafe formats; prefer data-only formats and integrity protection.
owasp: A08:2021 Software and Data Integrity Failures
verification_checks: Confirm the fix replaces the unsafe loader or adds signature verification and class restrictions.
false_positive_signals: Only safe loaders (json.loads, yaml.safe_load) are used on untrusted data.
remediation: Use safe, data-only formats (JSON) and safe loaders (yaml.safe_load).
Never deserialize untrusted data with pickle/unserialize/ObjectInputStream.
If unavoidable, sign and verify payloads (HMAC) and restrict deserialization to an allowlist of classes.

items.title: Server-Side Template Injection Remediation Guide
items.summary: Never compile templates from user input; pass untrusted data as bound variables and sandbox the engine.
verification_checks: Confirm user input is no longer concatenated into template source and is passed as data only.
false_positive_signals: Input is bound as a template variable, never compiled as template source.
remediation: Render fixed templates and pass user data only as bound context variables.
Do not build template strings from user input via from_string/render_template_string.
Where dynamic templates are required, use a sandboxed engine and restrict attribute access.

items.title: XXE Remediation Guide
items.summary: Disable DTD processing and external entity resolution on all XML parsers handling untrusted input.
verification_checks: Confirm the fix disables DTDs/external entities or switches to a hardened parser.
false_positive_signals: Parser already disables DTDs and external entity resolution (e.g. defusedxml).
remediation: Disable DTD processing and external general/parameter entities on the parser.
Use a hardened parser or library such as defusedxml.
Prefer non-XML formats or schema validation without entity expansion where possible.

items.title: IDOR / Broken Access Control Remediation Guide
items.summary: Enforce per-object ownership and authorization checks server-side for every request that references an identifier.
verification_checks: Confirm the fix ties each object access to the authenticated principal's permissions.
false_positive_signals: An ownership/role check already gates the object access.
remediation: Perform per-object authorization on the server using the session identity, not client-supplied IDs.
Prefer unguessable, session-scoped references over sequential identifiers.
For mass assignment, bind only an explicit allowlist of fields and never trust role/is_admin from the request.

items.title: Hardcoded Secret Remediation Guide
items.summary: Externalize secrets to a secrets manager or environment configuration, rotate exposed values, and purge them from version control.
verification_checks: Confirm the secret is removed from source, loaded from a secure store, and rotated.
false_positive_signals: The value is a placeholder or non-secret public identifier.
remediation: Load secrets from environment variables or a dedicated secrets manager (Vault, KMS, cloud secret store).
Rotate any secret that was committed, and revoke the exposed credential.
Purge the secret from VCS history and add pre-commit secret scanning to prevent recurrence.
